content.content:
1→// Package learning orchestrates the KB learning pipeline for error patterns and patches.
     2→//
     3→// This package provides the main learning orchestration for the Knowledge Base system,
     4→// coordinating between storage, fingerprinting, and model components to build
     5→// intelligent recommendations for automated error resolution.
     6→//
     7→// Key components:
     8→//   - KBLearner: Main learning pipeline orchestrator
     9→//   - Config: Learning behavior configuration (thresholds, limits, etc.)
    10→//   - Learning pipeline: Error processing, case management, summary updates
    11→//   - Recommendation engine: Best patch selection based on confidence scores
    12→//
    13→// Learning workflow:
    14→//  1. Process build errors and normalize to canonical signatures
    15→//  2. Deduplicate similar patches using semantic fingerprinting
    16→//  3. Update confidence scores based on historical success rates
    17→//  4. Maintain summary statistics for performance tracking
    18→//  5. Provide high-confidence patch recommendations
    19→//
    20→// Integration points:
    21→//   - Uses internal/kb/storage for data persistence
    22→//   - Uses internal/kb/fingerprint for patch similarity analysis
    23→//   - Uses internal/kb/models for all data structures
    24→//   - Used by transflow pipeline for automated healing
    25→//
    26→// See Also:
    27→//   - internal/kb/storage: For persistence operations
    28→//   - internal/kb/fingerprint: For patch analysis
    29→//   - internal/kb/models: For data structure definitions
    30→package learning
    31→
    32→import (
    33→	"context"
    34→	"fmt"
    35→	"log"
    36→	"time"
    37→
    38→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/kb/fingerprint"
    39→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/kb/models"
    40→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/kb/storage"
    41→)
    42→
    43→// KBLearner orchestrates the learning pipeline for error patterns and patches
    44→type KBLearner struct {
    45→	storage       *storage.KBStorage
    46→	fingerprinter *fingerprint.PatchFingerprinter
    47→	config        *Config
    48→}
    49→
    50→// Config configures the KB learning behavior
    51→type Config struct {
    52→	// Minimum confidence threshold for successful patches
    53→	MinConfidence float64
    54→
    55→	// Maximum number of cases to keep per error
    56→	MaxCasesPerError int
    57→
    58→	// Similarity threshold for case deduplication (0.0 - 1.0)
    59→	SimilarityThreshold float64
    60→
    61→	// Enable debug logging
    62→	EnableDebugLogging bool
    63→}
    64→
    65→// NewKBLearner creates a new KB learning pipeline
    66→func NewKBLearner(storage *storage.KBStorage, config *Config) *KBLearner {
    67→	if config == nil {
    68→		config = &Config{
    69→			MinConfidence:       0.7,
    70→			MaxCasesPerError:    50,
    71→			SimilarityThreshold: 0.8,
    72→			EnableDebugLogging:  false,
    73→		}
    74→	}
    75→
    76→	return &KBLearner{
    77→		storage:       storage,
    78→		fingerprinter: fingerprint.NewPatchFingerprinter(),
    79→		config:        config,
    80→	}
    81→}
    82→
    83→// LearnFromError processes a build error and attempts to learn from any provided patch
    84→func (kbl *KBLearner) LearnFromError(ctx context.Context, errorMsg string, location string, buildLogs []string, patch []byte, success bool) error {
    85→	// Generate canonical error signature
    86→	err := models.NewError(errorMsg, location, buildLogs)
    87→
    88→	// Store or update error in KB
    89→	existingError, retrieveErr := kbl.storage.RetrieveError(ctx, err.Signature)
    90→	if retrieveErr != nil {
    91→		// Error doesn't exist, store new one
    92→		if storeErr := kbl.storage.StoreError(ctx, err); storeErr != nil {
    93→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to store error: %w", storeErr)
    94→		}
    95→		kbl.debugLog("Stored new error: %s", err.ID)
    96→	} else {
    97→		err = existingError
    98→		kbl.debugLog("Found existing error: %s", err.ID)
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	// If no patch provided, just record the error
   102→	if len(patch) == 0 {
   103→		kbl.debugLog("No patch provided for error %s", err.ID)
   104→		return nil
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	// Process the patch and create a learning case
   108→	return kbl.processPatchCase(ctx, err, patch, success)
   109→}
   110→
   111→// processPatchCase processes a patch for an error and creates a learning case
   112→func (kbl *KBLearner) processPatchCase(ctx context.Context, err *models.Error, patch []byte, success bool) error {
   113→	// Generate patch fingerprint for logging
   114→	_ = kbl.fingerprinter.GenerateFingerprint(patch)
   115→
   116→	// Check for similar existing cases (deduplication)
   117→	existingCases, listErr := kbl.storage.ListCasesByError(ctx, err.ID)
   118→	if listErr != nil {
   119→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to list existing cases: %w", listErr)
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	// Find similar case for deduplication
   123→	similarCase := kbl.findSimilarCase(existingCases, patch)
   124→
   125→	if similarCase != nil {
   126→		// Update existing similar case
   127→		return kbl.updateSimilarCase(ctx, similarCase, success)
   128→	}
   129→
   130→	// Create new case using constructor
   131→	newCase := models.NewCase(err.ID, patch, success)
   132→	newCase.Confidence = kbl.calculateInitialConfidence(success)
   133→
   134→	if storeErr := kbl.storage.StoreCase(ctx, newCase); storeErr != nil {
   135→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to store case: %w", storeErr)
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	kbl.debugLog("Created new case %s for error %s (success: %t)", newCase.ID, err.ID, success)
   139→
   140→	// Update summary statistics
   141→	return kbl.updateErrorSummary(ctx, err.ID)
   142→}
   143→
   144→// findSimilarCase finds a case with similar patch content for deduplication
   145→func (kbl *KBLearner) findSimilarCase(cases []*models.Case, patch []byte) *models.Case {
   146→	for _, existingCase := range cases {
   147→		similarity := kbl.fingerprinter.CalculateSimilarity(existingCase.Patch, patch)
   148→		if similarity >= kbl.config.SimilarityThreshold {
   149→			kbl.debugLog("Found similar case with similarity %.2f", similarity)
   150→			return existingCase
   151→		}
   152→	}
   153→	return nil
   154→}
   155→
   156→// updateSimilarCase updates an existing case with new success/failure data
   157→func (kbl *KBLearner) updateSimilarCase(ctx context.Context, existingCase *models.Case, success bool) error {
   158→	// Update case confidence based on new outcome
   159→	// Create a pseudo-historical case for confidence calculation
   160→	historicalCases := []models.Case{*existingCase}
   161→	if success {
   162→		// Add a successful pseudo-case to improve confidence
   163→		successCase := models.Case{Success: true}
   164→		historicalCases = append(historicalCases, successCase)
   165→	} else {
   166→		// Add a failed pseudo-case to reduce confidence
   167→		failureCase := models.Case{Success: false}
   168→		historicalCases = append(historicalCases, failureCase)
   169→	}
   170→
   171→	// Update confidence based on new outcome
   172→	existingCase.UpdateConfidence(historicalCases)
   173→
   174→	kbl.debugLog("Updated case %s: new confidence %.2f",
   175→		existingCase.ID, existingCase.Confidence)
   176→
   177→	// Store updated case
   178→	return kbl.storage.StoreCase(ctx, existingCase)
   179→}
   180→
   181→// calculateInitialConfidence calculates initial confidence for a new case
   182→func (kbl *KBLearner) calculateInitialConfidence(success bool) float64 {
   183→	if success {
   184→		return 0.8 // High initial confidence for successful patches
   185→	}
   186→	return 0.2 // Low initial confidence for failed patches
   187→}
   188→
   189→// updateErrorSummary updates summary statistics for an error
   190→func (kbl *KBLearner) updateErrorSummary(ctx context.Context, errorID string) error {
   191→	cases, err := kbl.storage.ListCasesByError(ctx, errorID)
   192→	if err != nil {
   193→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to list cases for summary: %w", err)
   194→	}
   195→
   196→	if len(cases) == 0 {
   197→		return nil
   198→	}
   199→
   200→	// Calculate summary statistics
   201→	summary := kbl.calculateSummaryStats(errorID, cases)
   202→
   203→	// Store updated summary
   204→	if storeErr := kbl.storage.StoreSummary(ctx, summary); storeErr != nil {
   205→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to store summary: %w", storeErr)
   206→	}
   207→
   208→	kbl.debugLog("Updated summary for error %s: %d cases, %.1f%% success rate",
   209→		errorID, summary.CaseCount, summary.SuccessRate*100)
   210→
   211→	return nil
   212→}
   213→
   214→// calculateSummaryStats calculates summary statistics from cases
   215→func (kbl *KBLearner) calculateSummaryStats(errorID string, cases []*models.Case) *models.Summary {
   216→	successfulCases := 0
   217→	patchStats := make(map[string]*models.PatchSummary)
   218→
   219→	for _, c := range cases {
   220→		if c.Success && c.Confidence >= kbl.config.MinConfidence {
   221→			successfulCases++
   222→		}
   223→
   224→		// Track patch statistics
   225→		patchHash := c.PatchHash
   226→		if patchStats[patchHash] == nil {
   227→			patchStats[patchHash] = &models.PatchSummary{
   228→				Hash:         patchHash,
   229→				AttemptCount: 0,
   230→				SuccessRate:  0.0,
   231→			}
   232→		}
   233→
   234→		patchStats[patchHash].AttemptCount++
   235→	}
   236→
   237→	// Calculate success rates for patches
   238→	topPatches := make([]models.PatchSummary, 0, len(patchStats))
   239→	for hash, patchStat := range patchStats {
   240→		// Count successful cases for this patch hash
   241→		successCount := 0
   242→		for _, c := range cases {
   243→			if c.PatchHash == hash && c.Success {
   244→				successCount++
   245→			}
   246→		}
   247→
   248→		if patchStat.AttemptCount > 0 {
   249→			patchStat.SuccessRate = float64(successCount) / float64(patchStat.AttemptCount)
   250→		}
   251→		topPatches = append(topPatches, *patchStat)
   252→	}
   253→
   254→	// Sort patches by success rate (implementation would need sorting)
   255→	// For now, just take first few patches
   256→
   257→	return &models.Summary{
   258→		ErrorID:     errorID,
   259→		CaseCount:   len(cases),
   260→		SuccessRate: float64(successfulCases) / float64(len(cases)),
   261→		TopPatches:  topPatches,
   262→		Updated:     time.Now(),
   263→	}
   264→}
   265→
   266→// GetBestPatch retrieves the best patch recommendation for an error
   267→func (kbl *KBLearner) GetBestPatch(ctx context.Context, errorSignature string) (*models.Case, error) {
   268→	// Retrieve error by signature
   269→	err, retrieveErr := kbl.storage.RetrieveError(ctx, errorSignature)
   270→	if retrieveErr != nil {
   271→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error not found: %w", retrieveErr)
   272→	}
   273→
   274→	// Get all cases for this error
   275→	cases, listErr := kbl.storage.ListCasesByError(ctx, err.ID)
   276→	if listErr != nil {
   277→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list cases: %w", listErr)
   278→	}
   279→
   280→	if len(cases) == 0 {
   281→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no cases found for error")
   282→	}
   283→
   284→	// Find case with highest confidence above threshold
   285→	var bestCase *models.Case
   286→	highestConfidence := kbl.config.MinConfidence
   287→
   288→	for _, c := range cases {
   289→		if c.Success && c.Confidence > highestConfidence {
   290→			bestCase = c
   291→			highestConfidence = c.Confidence
   292→		}
   293→	}
   294→
   295→	if bestCase == nil {
   296→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no high-confidence patches available")
   297→	}
   298→
   299→	kbl.debugLog("Recommending patch with confidence %.2f for error %s",
   300→		bestCase.Confidence, err.ID)
   301→
   302→	return bestCase, nil
   303→}
   304→
   305→// debugLog logs debug messages if debug logging is enabled
   306→func (kbl *KBLearner) debugLog(format string, args ...interface{}) {
   307→	if kbl.config.EnableDebugLogging {
   308→		log.Printf("[KB-LEARNER] "+format, args...)
   309→	}
   310→}
   311→

content.text: Now let me examine some test files and security-critical areas:

content.content:
1→package vps
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"os"
     6→	"strings"
     7→	"testing"
     8→	"time"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    11→)
    12→
    13→func TestVPSProductionReadiness(t *testing.T) {
    14→	if os.Getenv("TARGET_HOST") == "" {
    15→		t.Skip("TARGET_HOST not set")
    16→	}
    17→
    18→	vpsClient := NewVPSClient(os.Getenv("TARGET_HOST"))
    19→
    20→	// Test production-like service topology
    21→	t.Run("ServiceTopology", func(t *testing.T) {
    22→		// Verify Consul cluster health
    23→		output, err := vpsClient.RunCommand("su - ploy -c 'consul members'")
    24→		assert.NoError(t, err)
    25→		assert.Contains(t, output, "alive", "Consul should be alive")
    26→
    27→		// Verify Nomad cluster health
    28→		output, err = vpsClient.RunCommand("su - ploy -c 'curl -f http://localhost:4646/v1/status/leader'")
    29→		assert.NoError(t, err)
    30→		assert.Contains(t, output, "127.0.0.1", "Nomad should have a leader")
    31→
    32→		// Verify SeaweedFS cluster health
    33→		output, err = vpsClient.RunCommand("su - ploy -c 'curl -s http://localhost:9333/cluster/status'")
    34→		assert.NoError(t, err)
    35→		assert.Contains(t, output, "Leader", "SeaweedFS should have leader")
    36→	})
    37→
    38→	// Test performance characteristics
    39→	t.Run("PerformanceBaseline", func(t *testing.T) {
    40→		// KB storage performance
    41→		start := time.Now()
    42→		testData := strings.Repeat("test", 1000) // 4KB test data
    43→
    44→		cmd := fmt.Sprintf(`su - ploy -c "echo '%s' | curl -w '%%{time_total}' -X POST http://localhost:8888/kb/perf/test -d @-"`, testData)
    45→		_, err := vpsClient.RunCommand(cmd)
    46→		assert.NoError(t, err)
    47→
    48→		duration := time.Since(start)
    49→		assert.True(t, duration < 2*time.Second, "KB storage should be responsive (<2s)")
    50→
    51→		// Nomad job submission performance
    52→		start = time.Now()
    53→		jobHCL := `
    54→job "perf-test" {
    55→  type = "batch"
    56→  group "test" {
    57→    task "echo" {
    58→      driver = "raw_exec"
    59→      config {
    60→        command = "echo"
    61→        args = ["performance test"]
    62→      }
    63→    }
    64→  }
    65→}
    66→`
    67→		jobFile := fmt.Sprintf("/tmp/perf-test-%d.hcl", time.Now().Unix())
    68→		writeCmd := fmt.Sprintf(`su - ploy -c "cat > %s << 'EOF'\n%s\nEOF"`, jobFile, jobHCL)
    69→		_, err = vpsClient.RunCommand(writeCmd)
    70→		assert.NoError(t, err)
    71→
    72→		submitCmd := fmt.Sprintf(`su - ploy -c "/opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh run --job perf-test --file %s"`, jobFile)
    73→		_, err = vpsClient.RunCommand(submitCmd)
    74→		assert.NoError(t, err)
    75→
    76→		duration = time.Since(start)
    77→		assert.True(t, duration < 30*time.Second, "Job submission should be fast (<30s)")
    78→
    79→		// Cleanup
    80→		vpsClient.RunCommand(fmt.Sprintf(`su - ploy -c "/opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh stop --job perf-test"`))
    81→		vpsClient.RunCommand(fmt.Sprintf(`su - ploy -c "rm -f %s"`, jobFile))
    82→	})
    83→}
    84→
    85→func TestVPSTransflowEndToEnd(t *testing.T) {
    86→	if os.Getenv("TARGET_HOST") == "" {
    87→		t.Skip("TARGET_HOST not set")
    88→	}
    89→
    90→	vpsClient := NewVPSClient(os.Getenv("TARGET_HOST"))
    91→
    92→	t.Run("TransflowWorkflowValidation", func(t *testing.T) {
    93→		// Test transflow CLI is available and shows correct help
    94→		configCmd := `su - ploy -c "/opt/ploy/bin/ploy transflow --help"`
    95→		output, err := vpsClient.RunCommand(configCmd)
    96→		assert.NoError(t, err, "Transflow CLI should be accessible")
    97→		assert.Contains(t, output, "transflow.yaml", "Transflow should show usage")
    98→
    99→		// Test configuration file exists
   100→		fileCmd := `su - ploy -c "test -f /opt/ploy/test/fixtures/java-migration.yaml && echo 'config exists'"`
   101→		output, err = vpsClient.RunCommand(fileCmd)
   102→		assert.NoError(t, err, "Should be able to check config file")
   103→		assert.Contains(t, output, "config exists", "Transflow config file should exist")
   104→
   105→		// Test KB learning system availability
   106→		kbTestCmd := `su - ploy -c "curl -f http://localhost:8888/kb/cases/"`
   107→		_, err = vpsClient.RunCommand(kbTestCmd)
   108→		assert.NoError(t, err, "KB learning system should be accessible")
   109→	})
   110→
   111→	t.Run("VPSEnvironmentVariables", func(t *testing.T) {
   112→		// Verify required environment variables are set
   113→		envVars := []string{"CONSUL_HTTP_ADDR", "NOMAD_ADDR", "SEAWEEDFS_MASTER", "SEAWEEDFS_FILER"}
   114→
   115→		for _, envVar := range envVars {
   116→			cmd := fmt.Sprintf(`su - ploy -c "echo \\$%s"`, envVar)
   117→			output, err := vpsClient.RunCommand(cmd)
   118→			assert.NoError(t, err, "Should be able to read environment variable %s", envVar)
   119→			assert.NotEmpty(t, strings.TrimSpace(output), "Environment variable %s should be set", envVar)
   120→		}
   121→	})
   122→}
   123→
   124→func TestVPSSecurityAndAccess(t *testing.T) {
   125→	if os.Getenv("TARGET_HOST") == "" {
   126→		t.Skip("TARGET_HOST not set")
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	vpsClient := NewVPSClient(os.Getenv("TARGET_HOST"))
   130→
   131→	t.Run("AccessControls", func(t *testing.T) {
   132→		// Verify ploy user exists and has correct permissions
   133→		output, err := vpsClient.RunCommand("id ploy")
   134→		assert.NoError(t, err, "ploy user should exist")
   135→		assert.Contains(t, output, "ploy", "ploy user should be in output")
   136→
   137→		// Verify directory permissions
   138→		output, err = vpsClient.RunCommand("su - ploy -c 'ls -la /opt/ploy'")
   139→		assert.NoError(t, err, "Should be able to list /opt/ploy directory as ploy user")
   140→		assert.Contains(t, output, "bin", "/opt/ploy should contain bin directory")
   141→
   142→		// Verify transflow binary is executable by ploy user
   143→		output, err = vpsClient.RunCommand("su - ploy -c 'test -x /opt/ploy/bin/ploy && echo \"executable\"'")
   144→		assert.NoError(t, err, "Should be able to test ploy binary executability")
   145→		assert.Contains(t, output, "executable", "ploy binary should be executable")
   146→	})
   147→
   148→	t.Run("ServiceSecurity", func(t *testing.T) {
   149→		// Test that services are not exposed externally (should only be accessible locally)
   150→		services := map[string]string{
   151→			"consul":           "8500",
   152→			"nomad":            "4646",
   153→			"seaweedfs-master": "9333",
   154→			"seaweedfs-filer":  "8888",
   155→		}
   156→
   157→		for service, port := range services {
   158→			// Test that service is accessible locally
   159→			localCmd := fmt.Sprintf(`su - ploy -c "curl -f http://localhost:%s/ || curl -f http://localhost:%s/v1/status/leader"`, port, port)
   160→			_, err := vpsClient.RunCommand(localCmd)
   161→			assert.NoError(t, err, "Service %s should be accessible locally on port %s", service, port)
   162→		}
   163→	})
   164→}
   165→
